meta.note: National exam statistics straight from each credentialing body. First-time pass rates unless the label says otherwise. Reality data on the credential, never odds on a person.

nclex-rn.chain: CNA or PCT → LPN or ADN → NCLEX-RN → BSN → specialty and graduate tracks

entry.note: Entrance exams and prereqs are set per program; TEAS and HESI A2 are the common asks.

nclex-pn.chain: CNA → 12-month PN program → NCLEX-PN → LPN-to-RN bridge

entry.note: Admissions are set per program; most PN programs run through community and technical colleges.

aanp-fnp.length: MSN or DNP, 2 to 4 years past the RN
aanp-fnp.chain: RN → BSN → MSN or DNP (NP track) → AANPCB or ANCC boards
aanp-fnp.note: The FNP exam, the highest-volume NP certification. DNP-prepared candidates passed at 88%.

entry.note: Many programs prefer 1-2 years of RN experience; RN-to-MSN bridge entries exist for ADN nurses.

nbcrna-nce.length: 3-year doctorate after a BSN plus ICU years
nbcrna-nce.chain: BSN → 1+ years ICU → doctoral anesthesia program → NCE
nbcrna-nce.note: 2,740 first-time candidates in 2024, up from 84.9% in 2023.

entry.hours: 1+ year full-time as an RN in a critical care setting (COA minimum; ICU-type units)
entry.note: Many programs prefer or require CCRN certification; all entry programs are now doctoral.

amcb-cnm.chain: RN → BSN → graduate midwifery program → AMCB exam
amcb-cnm.note: Repeat attempts pass at 51%. The first try matters.

entry.note: All ACME-accredited programs require a bachelor's for entry; non-nurses can enter via accelerated BSN bridge pathways.

ccrn.length: not a program; 1,750 ICU hours in 2 years

entry.hours: 1,750 hrs of direct critical-care practice in the past 2 yrs (875 in the most recent yr), or 2,000 hrs over 5 yrs
entry.note: Requires a current unencumbered US RN or APRN license; hours must be verifiable bedside critical-care practice.

cna.chain: 75+ hour state course → state exam → nurse aide registry
cna.note: No national pass rate is published. The exam and fee (typically $100 to $160) are set state by state.

entry.note: Federal floor is a 75-hour state-approved course (incl. 16 supervised practical hrs); many states require more. Find approved courses through your state nurse aide registry.

arrt-r.chain: 2-year radiography program → ARRT (R) → post-primary CT, MRI, mammo, IR
arrt-r.note: Radiography primary pathway. The post-primary modality exams have their own rates.

entry.note: JRCERT accredits programs but doesn't set admission rules; prereqs vary by program and some also require an entrance exam (TEAS/HESI A2) or job-shadowing hours.

ardms-spi.chain: Sonography program → SPI physics + a specialty exam (Abdomen, OB/GYN, echo)
ardms-spi.note: Specialty exams run $300 each; Abdomen passed at 73% first-time. Link is the archived copy of ARDMS's own 2024 summary; the live PDF moved in the Inteleos site migration.

entry.note: 1-year programs require prior allied-health/patient-care qualifications; 2-year programs take high-school grads with a basic science, physics, and math background.

ascp-mls.chain: MLT plus a bridge, or a 4-year MLS program → ASCP MLS
ascp-mls.note: From the ASCP BOC 2024 pass-rate report (archived copy; the live PDF sits behind a hotlink block).

entry.note: 4-year MLS degrees build the sciences in; upper-division and post-bacc programs require these as admission prereqs, and each program sets its own.

ascp-mlt.chain: 2-year NAACLS program → ASCP MLT → MLS bridge
ascp-mlt.note: From the ASCP BOC 2024 pass-rate report (archived copy; the live PDF sits behind a hotlink block).

entry.note: MLT associate programs set their own prereqs; these are the common asks and many are taken early in the same degree.

nha-cpt.note: NHA reports all attempts, not first-time. ASCP's phlebotomy exam (PBT) ran 91% first-time in 2024.

entry.note: Entry usually needs only a high-school diploma or GED; the 30 venipuncture/10 skin-puncture minimum is for the exam, earned during training.

nremt-emt.chain: One-semester EMT course → NREMT → AEMT or paramedic program
nremt-emt.note: 2024 was 74%; the exam changed in April 2025.

prereqs: CPR/BLS, often earned early in the course
entry.note: Course entry rules are set by states and individual schools, not NREMT, so check your state EMS office before enrolling.

nremt-p.chain: EMT → 1-to-2-year paramedic program → NREMT-P → flight and critical care certs

prereqs: Current EMT certification or state license
entry.hours: Many programs want 6-18 months of EMT field experience first; some accept new EMTs (varies by program)
entry.note: The EMT card is the real gate: current EMT-level certification is required for the paramedic pathway and at program admission.

nbstsa-cst.chain: Surgical tech program → CST → surgical first assist (CSFA) with experience

entry.note: NBSTSA only requires graduating a CAAHEP or ABHES accredited program; each college sets its own admissions on top, often adding science prereqs or a placement test.

hspa-crcst.note: HSPA publishes raw counts, not a rate: 10,083 passed and 5,286 failed in 2025, about 66%, retakes included.

prereqs: None required by HSPA; optional prep course or self-study
entry.note: There is no entry gate: no course, degree, or diploma is required to start, so the 400 hands-on hours are the whole road.
CCI also offers experience-based pathways that skip accredited-program entry entirely if you already work in a cath lab.

nbrc-tmc.chain: CoARC program → TMC low cut (CRT) → TMC high cut + CSE (RRT) → ACCS, NPS, SDS
nbrc-tmc.note: The RRT bar is higher: 69.9% cleared the high cut, and 62.9% passed the CSE ($200) in 2024.

entry.note: CoARC programs set their own admission prereqs; these are common asks, and some programs also require an entrance exam (TEAS/HESI A2).

nccpa-pance.chain: Bachelor's + patient-care hours → PA program → PANCE

prereqs: Science prerequisites (A&P, chemistry, biology, micro common)
entry.hours: Most programs expect hands-on patient-care hours; minimums vary by program, matriculant median ~2,900 hrs (PAEA)
entry.note: Apply through CASPA; every program sets its own hour minimums and exam list, so check each one.

exams: GRE (program-dependent; many DPT programs have dropped it)
entry.hours: PT observation/volunteer hours under a licensed PT commonly required; amount set per program
entry.note: Bachelor's degree comes first; PTCAS lists each program's exact prereqs, GRE policy, and hour minimums.

entry.hours: Many programs want volunteer/observation time in a PT clinic, often with a PT reference letter
entry.note: No standard PTA admission requirements; you apply directly to each associate-degree program (about 2 years).

nbcot-otr.chain: Bachelor's → MSOT or OTD → NBCOT OTR
nbcot-otr.note: Counting retakes, 92% of 2025 grads passed within a year of graduating.

exams: GRE (some programs; many have dropped it)
entry.hours: Many programs require documented OT observation hours; amounts set per program
entry.note: Bachelor's in any major works; prerequisites vary significantly by program, so check each program's list in OTCAS.

nbcot-cota.note: Counting retakes, 85% of 2025 grads passed within a year.

entry.note: Associate-degree OTA programs admit directly through each college; admission criteria are program-specific.

praxis-slp.chain: Bachelor's → MS-SLP → Praxis + clinical fellowship → CCC-SLP
praxis-slp.note: No national first-time rate is published; this counts retakes.

exams: GRE (some programs; many no longer require it)
prereqs: CSD foundational coursework (leveling year if non-CSD major)
entry.note: A bachelor's in any major works, but non-CSD majors usually complete leveling prerequisites first.

naplex.chain: 2+ years prerequisites → 4-year PharmD → NAPLEX + state law exam
naplex.note: Rebounded from 75.9% for 2024 grads after the exam redesign.

entry.note: Most students enter after 3+ years of college; prerequisites are required but a bachelor's usually isn't.

ptcb-ptce.chain: Training program or 500 work hours → PTCE → CPhT
ptcb-ptce.note: PTCB publishes one yearly rate, not a first-time breakout.

prereqs: High school diploma/GED (typical for training program enrollment)
entry.hours: None to enter training; 500 hrs of pharmacy work experience is an alternate path to exam eligibility
entry.note: PTCB recognizes accredited and attested programs; the work-experience path skips school entirely.

aapc-cpc.chain: Coding course → CPC-A apprentice → CPC after 2 years of experience
aapc-cpc.note: AAPC publishes no national pass rate. The numbers you see online come from test-prep marketing.

prereqs: No formal prerequisites; HS diploma typical
Anatomy + medical terminology strongly recommended first
entry.hours: None to train or test; new CPCs hold CPC-A (apprentice) until 2 yrs experience, or 1 yr plus an 80-hr course
entry.note: AAPC recommends but does not require a degree; people from clinical roles can usually skip the anatomy/med-term primers.

ahima-rhit.chain: 2-year CAHIIM associate → RHIT → RHIA with a bachelor's

prereqs: High school diploma/GED (standard community college admission)
entry.note: The program must be HIM specifically; CAHIIM-accredited health informatics degrees do not qualify for the RHIT exam.

ahima-rhia.chain: RHIT or a CAHIIM bachelor's → RHIA

entry.note: Already hold a bachelor's? A CAHIIM-approved post-baccalaureate HIM certificate or master's-level HIM program also qualifies for the RHIA exam.

boc-atc.chain: Bachelor's → CAATE master's → BOC exam → state license

entry.note: Bachelor's required; CAATE standards mandate these science prereqs but exact courses are set per program.

cdr-rdn.chain: ACEND program + master's → supervised practice → CDR exam
cdr-rdn.note: 83.9% pass within a year of the first attempt.

entry.note: Since 2024 a graduate degree is required for exam eligibility; typical routes run through an ACEND graduate program with 1,000+ supervised experiential hours.
